2603.17303.title: From Words to Worlds: Benchmarking Cross-Cultural Cultural Understanding in Machine Translation
2603.17303.paper_summary: Culture-expressions, such as idioms, slang, and culture-specific items (CSIs), are pervasive in natural language and encode meanings that go beyond literal linguistic form. Accurately translating such expressions remains challenging for machine translation systems. Despite this, existing benchmarks remain fragmented and do not provide a systematic framework for evaluating translation performance on culture-loaded expressions. To address this gap, we introduce CulT-Eval, a benchmark designed to evaluate how models handle different types of culturally grounded expressions. CulT-Eval comprises over 7,959 carefully curated instances spanning multiple types of culturally grounded expressions, with a comprehensive error taxonomy covering culturally grounded expressions. Through extensive evaluation of large language models and detailed analysis, we identify recurring and systematic failure modes that are not adequately captured by existing automatic metrics. Accordingly, we propose a complementary evaluation metric that targets culturally induced meaning deviations overlooked by standard MT metrics. The results indicate that current models struggle to preserve culturally grounded meaning and to capture the cultural and contextual nuances essential for accurate translation. Our benchmark and code are available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CulT-Eval-E75D/.
2603.17303.paper_summary_zh: 文化表達，如成語、俚語和文化特定項目（CSIs），在自然語言中無處不在，並編碼超越字面語言形式的意義。準確翻譯這些表達對機器翻譯系統來說仍然具有挑戰性。儘管如此，現有的基準仍然是零散的，並未提供系統化的框架來評估文化負載表達的翻譯表現。為了解決這一空白，我們介紹了CulT-Eval，這是一個旨在評估模型如何處理不同類型文化根植表達的基準。CulT-Eval包含超過7,959個精心策劃的實例，涵蓋多種類型的文化根植表達，並有一個全面的錯誤分類法，涵蓋文化根植表達。通過對大型語言模型的廣泛評估和詳細分析，我們識別出現有自動度量未能充分捕捉的重複性和系統性失敗模式。因此，我們提出了一個補充評估指標，針對標準機器翻譯指標忽略的文化引起的意義偏差。結果顯示，當前模型在保持文化根植意義和捕捉準確翻譯所需的文化及上下文細微差別方面存在困難。我們的基準和代碼可在https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CulT-Eval-E75D/獲得。
2603.17303.author: Bangju Han et.al.
2603.17303.authors: Bangju Han, Yingqi Wang, Huang Qing, Tiyuan Li, Fengyi Yang, Ahtamjan Ahmat, Abibulla Atawulla, Yating Yang, Xi Zhou
